paragraphs.en: Free Expression is a daily newsletter on American life, politics and culture published by the Opinion pages of The Wall Street Journal.
paragraphs.cn: 《自由表达》是《华尔街日报》评论版每日发布的一份关于美国生活、政治和文化的时事通讯。

paragraphs.en: We believe in democratic self-government and the freedom to make your own economic choices, the values that have animated the work of the Journal editorial page since Charles Dow set them out 130 years ago.
paragraphs.cn: 我们相信民主自治和自由做出经济选择的权利，这些价值观自查尔斯·道130年前确立以来，一直激励着《华尔街日报》社论版的工作。

paragraphs.en: But we understand that our readers are—and have always been—more than rational economic beings or politically committed partisans. They are complicated and curious. They seek knowledge and nuance. Above all, they want to listen to an argument and decide for themselves.
paragraphs.cn: 但我们明白，我们的读者——也一直都是——不仅仅是理性的经济个体或政治上坚定的党派人士。他们复杂而好奇。他们寻求知识和细微之处。最重要的是，他们希望倾听论点并自行做出决定。

paragraphs.en: The Free Expression newsletter doesn’t ignore what goes on in Washington or on Wall Street, but looks beyond—to the culture and conversations that make this country unique. We have our views, and will state them forcefully, but are happy to hear from those who disagree.
paragraphs.cn: 《自由表达》时事通讯不忽视华盛顿或华尔街发生的事情，而是放眼更远——关注塑造这个国家独特性的文化和对话。我们有自己的观点，并将强有力地表达出来，但也乐于倾听不同意见。

paragraphs.en: Free Expression is a place where differences can sort themselves out through discussion, making us all better off even if we don’t end up seeing eye-to-eye.
paragraphs.cn: 《自由表达》是一个可以通过讨论解决分歧的地方，即使我们最终无法完全达成一致，也能让我们所有人受益。

paragraphs.en: Matthew Hennessey is editor of Free Expression. Previously he was the Journal’s deputy editorial features editor, overseeing the flow of columnist and contributor copy on the daily op-ed pages. Before joining the Journal in 2017, he was an editor at City Journal. A native of Morristown, N.J., he is the author of two books, “Zero Hour for Gen X” and “Visible Hand: A Wealth of Notions on the Miracle of the Market.” He is a graduate of CUNY-Hunter College and Fordham University. @matthennessey
paragraphs.cn: 马修·亨尼西是《自由表达》的编辑。此前，他曾担任《华尔街日报》副社论特稿编辑，负责每日评论版上专栏作家和撰稿人稿件的流程。在2017年加入《华尔街日报》之前，他曾是《城市杂志》的编辑。他出生于新泽西州莫里斯敦，是两本书的作者：《X世代的零时》和《看得见的手：关于市场奇迹的丰富观念》。他毕业于纽约市立大学亨特学院和福特汉姆大学。@matthennessey

paragraphs.en: Jack Butler is deputy editor of Free Expression. He joined the Journal in 2025 from National Review, where for four years he was submissions editor for National Review Online. Before that, he spent a year as an associate editor at the magazine and was a research assistant for Jonah Goldberg at the American Enterprise Institute. He was a 2022-23 Robert Novak Fellow. He is a graduate of Hillsdale College and was one of the top 250 finishers of the 2019 Boston Marathon. @JackButler4815
paragraphs.cn: 杰克·巴特勒是《自由表达》的副编辑。他于2025年从《国家评论》加入《华尔街日报》，此前他在《国家评论在线》担任投稿编辑四年。在此之前，他曾在该杂志担任一年副主编，并在美国企业研究所担任乔纳·戈德堡的研究助理。他是2022-23年度罗伯特·诺瓦克研究员。他毕业于希尔斯代尔学院，是2019年波士顿马拉松赛前250名完赛者之一。@JackButler4815

paragraphs.en: Mary Julia Koch is associate editor of Free Expression. She joined the Journal in 2025 from the New York Sun, where she covered higher education and foreign policy. Her reporting has also appeared in the Free Press, Air Mail and the Republic. A 2023 graduate of Harvard College, she served as editor-in-chief of the weekly Harvard Independent. @MJ_Koch
paragraphs.cn: 玛丽·朱莉娅·科赫是《自由表达》的副编辑。她于2025年从《纽约太阳报》加入《华尔街日报》，在那里她负责高等教育和外交政策报道。她的报道也曾出现在《自由报》、《航空邮件》和《共和国》上。她于2023年毕业于哈佛学院，曾担任每周出版的《哈佛独立报》主编。@MJ_Koch

paragraphs.en: Emma Camp is a senior newsletter editor at Free Expression. She joined the Journal in 2026 from Reason magazine. Her essays have appeared in the New York Times, the Atlantic, Slate, America and Persuasion. She is a 2025-26 Robert Novak Fellow and her book “Good Boundaries,” on Gen Z and risk-aversion, will be published by Ballantine in 2027. She is a graduate of the University of Virginia. @emmma_camp_
paragraphs.cn: 艾玛·坎普是《自由表达》的高级时事通讯编辑。她于2026年从《理性》杂志加入《华尔街日报》。她的文章曾发表在《纽约时报》、《大西洋月刊》、《Slate》、《美国》和《说服》上。她是2025-26年度罗伯特·诺瓦克研究员，她的关于Z世代和风险规避的书《好的界限》将于2027年由巴兰汀出版社出版。她毕业于弗吉尼亚大学。@emmma_camp_

paragraphs.en: Matthew Continetti is a Free Expression columnist at WSJ Opinion. He is also the Patrick and Charlene Neal Chair in American Prosperity and director of domestic policy studies at the American Enterprise Institute. A graduate of Columbia University, he’s been an opinion editor of the Weekly Standard, the founding editor of the Washington Free Beacon and a columnist for Commentary and the Free Press. He appears regularly on “Special Report with Bret Baier” on Fox News Channel. His most recent book is “The Right: The Hundred-Year War for American Conservatism.” @continetti
paragraphs.cn: 马修·康蒂内蒂是《华尔街日报》评论版的《自由表达》专栏作家。他还是美国企业研究所的帕特里克和查琳·尼尔美国繁荣讲席教授，以及国内政策研究主任。他毕业于哥伦比亚大学，曾担任《每周标准》的评论编辑、《华盛顿自由灯塔报》的创始编辑，以及《评论》和《自由报》的专栏作家。他经常出现在福克斯新闻频道的“布雷特·拜尔特别报道”节目中。他最近的著作是《右翼：美国保守主义百年战争》。@continetti

paragraphs.en: Meghan Cox Gurdon is a Free Expression columnist at WSJ Opinion. She also writes a weekly column for the Journal’s Books pages and is the author of “The Enchanted Hour: The Miraculous Power of Reading Aloud in the Age of Distraction,” which will soon be available in eight languages. A former foreign correspondent and graduate of Bowdoin College, she has five children with her husband, the English journalist Hugo Gurdon. They divide their time between Maryland and Maine. @MeghanGurdon
paragraphs.cn: 梅根·考克斯·格登是《华尔街日报》评论版的《自由表达》专栏作家。她还为《华尔街日报》图书版撰写每周专栏，并且是《魔法时刻：分心时代朗读的奇迹力量》一书的作者，该书即将以八种语言出版。她曾是一名外国记者，毕业于鲍登学院，与她的英国记者丈夫雨果·格登育有五个孩子。他们将时间分配在马里兰州和缅因州之间。@MeghanGurdon

paragraphs.en: James B. Meigs is a Free Expression columnist at WSJ Opinion. He has been editor-in-chief of several magazines, including Popular Mechanics. He helped launch and is the former co-host of the “How Do We Fix It?” podcast, which highlights common-ground solutions to policy problems. He frequently appears on the Discovery television network as a commentator on the history of technology. He has also served as a technology columnist for Commentary magazine and a contributing editor at City Journal. His articles have appeared in the New York Post, the New York Times, Slate and other publications. He is a senior fellow at the Manhattan Institute and graduated from Dartmouth College with a B.A. in philosophy. @jamesbmeigs
paragraphs.cn: 詹姆斯·B·梅格斯是《华尔街日报》评论版的《自由表达》专栏作家。他曾担任多本杂志的主编，包括《大众机械》。他帮助创办并曾是“我们如何解决？”播客的联合主持人，该播客重点关注政策问题的共同解决方案。他经常作为技术史评论员出现在探索电视频道。他还曾担任《评论》杂志的技术专栏作家和《城市杂志》的特约编辑。他的文章曾发表在《纽约邮报》、《纽约时报》、《Slate》和其他出版物上。他是曼哈顿研究所的高级研究员，毕业于达特茅斯学院，获得哲学学士学位。@jamesbmeigs

paragraphs.en: John J. Miller is a Free Expression columnist at WSJ Opinion. He is also director of the Dow Journalism Program at Hillsdale College and the founder and executive director of the Student Free Press Association, a nonprofit group best known for its news website, the College Fix. His books include “Reading Around: Journalism on Authors, Artists, and Ideas,” “The Big Scrum: How Teddy Roosevelt Saved Football” and “The First Assassin,” a historical thriller set during the Civil War. He was a longtime writer and podcaster for National Review. He lives on a dirt road in rural Michigan. @heymiller
paragraphs.cn: 约翰·J·米勒是《华尔街日报》评论版的《自由表达》专栏作家。他还是希尔斯代尔学院道氏新闻学项目的主任，以及学生自由新闻协会的创始人兼执行董事，该协会是一个以其新闻网站“大学修正”而闻名的非营利组织。他的著作包括《阅读：关于作者、艺术家和思想的新闻学》、《大争夺：西奥多·罗斯福如何拯救橄榄球》和《第一刺客》，一部以美国内战为背景的历史惊悚小说。他曾是《国家评论》的长期撰稿人和播客主持人。他住在密歇根州乡村的一条土路上。@heymiller

paragraphs.en: Louise Perry is a Free Expression columnist at WSJ Opinion. Her bestselling debut book, “The Case Against the Sexual Revolution,” was published in 2022. She has previously worked at publications including the Daily Mail, the New Statesman and UnHerd. She hosts Maiden Mother Matriarch, a podcast about culture and politics. @Louise_m_perry
paragraphs.cn: 路易丝·佩里是《华尔街日报》评论版的《自由表达》专栏作家。她的畅销处女作《反对性革命的案例》于2022年出版。她曾供职于《每日邮报》、《新政治家》和《UnHerd》等出版物。她主持播客“少女、母亲、女族长”，该播客讨论文化和政治。@Louise_m_perry

paragraphs.en: Ben Sasse is a Free Expression columnist at WSJ Opinion. From 2015-23 he was a Republican U.S. senator from Nebraska serving on the intelligence, finance and judiciary committees. He is president emeritus of the University of Florida, where he also teaches. As a historian, he has won teaching awards and holds a Ph.D. from Yale. He and his wife, Melissa, have three children and currently reside in Gainesville, Fla., but their hearts will always be on the Platte River in Nebraska. @BenSasse
paragraphs.cn: 本·萨斯是《华尔街日报》评论版的《自由表达》专栏作家。2015年至2023年，他曾担任内布拉斯加州的共和党美国参议员，并在情报、财政和司法委员会任职。他是佛罗里达大学的荣誉校长，也在那里任教。作为一名历史学家，他曾获得教学奖项，并拥有耶鲁大学博士学位。他和妻子梅丽莎育有三个孩子，目前居住在佛罗里达州盖恩斯维尔，但他们的心永远在内布拉斯加州的普拉特河畔。@BenSasse

paragraphs.en: Kyle Smith is a Free Expression columnist and the Journal’s film critic. He grew up in East Longmeadow, Mass., and graduated from Yale College, which he attended on an ROTC scholarship. He served as a lieutenant in the U.S. Army in the Persian Gulf War. He has previously written for the New York Post and National Review and is the author of the comic novels “Love Monkey” and “A Christmas Caroline.” He lives on the Upper West Side of Manhattan with his family, 3,000 books and a fluffy little dog. @rkylesmith
paragraphs.cn: 凯尔·史密斯是《自由表达》专栏作家和《华尔街日报》的电影评论员。他在马萨诸塞州东长草地长大，毕业于耶鲁大学，他通过ROTC奖学金就读。他曾在美国陆军服役，在波斯湾战争中担任中尉。他曾为《纽约邮报》和《国家评论》撰稿，是喜剧小说《爱情猴子》和《圣诞卡罗琳》的作者。他与家人、3000本书和一只毛茸茸的小狗住在曼哈顿上西区。

vocabulary.meaning_en: A report or bulletin issued periodically to the members of a society, club, or other organization, or to customers of a business.
vocabulary.example: Free Expression is a daily newsletter on American life, politics and culture published by the Opinion pages of The Wall Street Journal.
vocabulary.example_cn: 《自由表达》是《华尔街日报》评论版每日发布的一份关于美国生活、政治和文化的时事通讯。

vocabulary.meaning_en: Full of life or excitement; lively. (In this context: gave life or spirit to)
vocabulary.meaning_cn: 充满活力的；（在此处引申为）激励的
vocabulary.example: We believe in democratic self-government and the freedom to make your own economic choices, the values that have animated the work of the Journal editorial page since Charles Dow set them out 130 years ago.
vocabulary.example_cn: 我们相信民主自治和自由做出经济选择的权利，这些价值观自查尔斯·道130年前确立以来，一直激励着《华尔街日报》社论版的工作。

vocabulary.meaning_en: A strong supporter of a party, cause, or person.
vocabulary.example: But we understand that our readers are—and have always been—more than rational economic beings or politically committed partisans.
vocabulary.example_cn: 但我们明白，我们的读者——也一直都是——不仅仅是理性的经济个体或政治上坚定的党派人士。

vocabulary.meaning_en: A subtle difference in or shade of meaning, expression, or sound.
vocabulary.example_cn: 他们寻求知识和细微之处。

vocabulary.meaning_en: In a strong, assertive, or vigorous way.
vocabulary.example: We have our views, and will state them forcefully, but are happy to hear from those who disagree.
vocabulary.example_cn: 我们有自己的观点，并将强有力地表达出来，但也乐于倾听不同意见。

vocabulary.meaning_en: Supervise (a person or their work), especially in an official capacity.
vocabulary.example: Previously he was the Journal’s deputy editorial features editor, overseeing the flow of columnist and contributor copy on the daily op-ed pages.
vocabulary.example_cn: 此前，他曾担任《华尔街日报》副社论特稿编辑，负责每日评论版上专栏作家和撰稿人稿件的流程。

vocabulary.meaning_en: The act of presenting a proposal, application, or other document for consideration or judgment. (In this context: articles or content submitted for publication)
vocabulary.example: He joined the Journal in 2025 from National Review, where for four years he was submissions editor for National Review Online.
vocabulary.example_cn: 他于2025年从《国家评论》加入《华尔街日报》，此前他在《国家评论在线》担任投稿编辑四年。

vocabulary.meaning_en: A journalist who writes a regular column in a newspaper or magazine.
vocabulary.example: Matthew Continetti is a Free Expression columnist at WSJ Opinion.
vocabulary.example_cn: 马修·康蒂内蒂是《华尔街日报》评论版的《自由表达》专栏作家。

vocabulary.meaning_en: A person who is in charge of an organization or department.
vocabulary.example: He is also the Patrick and Charlene Neal Chair in American Prosperity and director of domestic policy studies at the American Enterprise Institute.
vocabulary.example_cn: 他还是美国企业研究所的帕特里克和查琳·尼尔美国繁荣讲席教授，以及国内政策研究主任。

vocabulary.meaning_en: Not making or conducted primarily to make a profit.
vocabulary.example: He is also director of the Dow Journalism Program at Hillsdale College and the founder and executive director of the Student Free Press Association, a nonprofit group best known for its news website, the College Fix.
vocabulary.example_cn: 他还是希尔斯代尔学院道氏新闻学项目的主任，以及学生自由新闻协会的创始人兼执行董事，该协会是一个以其新闻网站“大学修正”而闻名的非营利组织。
